object.description: Collateral p27 readings in British Library, Harley MS 1704.

subject.description: Horstmann p27 textual apparatus.

reviewNotes: The frozen packet's 49 abstract compilation-to-workpart relations project to metadata content_parts because the lineage schema intentionally has no abstract-work entity type.
The packet's conceptual de Worde group and two grouping relations are omitted; the exact 1506 and 1508 print states remain independent entities and primary sources.
Allen and Lamothe relations are transformed into schema-valid supporting descriptions and never appear in a primary entity sequence.

Three additional evidence-backed facsimile relationships expose the selected Lincoln f. 80r image and the complete 1975/1977 Scolar Thornton reproductions without pretending their catalog pages are the facsimiles themselves.

Thus the schema projection contains 171 primary relations and 15 supporting relations; the four apparatus-only p27 collations have no duplicated repository/U-M edges, and every final relation is classified exactly once.

lineage.summary: The repository XML copies the 2003 U-M companion encoding, which selects and encodes Carl Horstmann's 1895-1896 Yorkshire Writers text. Fifty-seven primary item/source streams connect exactly forty-nine intellectual items to twelve manuscript witnesses and three early-print carriers; p27 apparatus collations, facsimiles, collateral editions, catalogs, and modern scholarship remain supporting relationships.

catalogSummary.author: Multiple anonymous and differently attributed authors (not blanket Richard Rolle)

catalogSummary.region: England; northern, West Midlands, and southern dialect/witness associations; London early-print carriers
catalogSummary.title: Companion to the English Prose Works of Richard Rolle: a Selection from Carl Horstmann's Edition

catalogingSubject.label: Companion to the English Prose Works of Richard Rolle: a Selection from Carl Horstmann's Edition
catalogingSubject.scope_note: A University of Michigan/CME electronic companion selection, encoded in 2003 from Carl Horstmann's 1895-1896 two-volume Yorkshire Writers edition. It is not a single medieval work and is not a collected-works container whose forty-nine intellectual items may be blanket-attributed to Richard Rolle. The cataloging boundary is 7 editorial groups, 44 DIV2 containers, and exactly 49 intellectual items; the four split containers remain explicit.

First of two independent intellectual works in DIV2 7.5. Horstmann's p. 389 is editorial introduction; the work text on p. 390 is printed from Royal 17 A XXV f. 62, with Harley 1706 ff. 54v-66v and other copies collateral. Allen notes a probable connection with a similar German piece circulating with Albert of Cologne.

extent.description: Seven editorial groups and forty-four encoded DIV2 containers map to forty-nine count-once intellectual items. Forty-seven are prose-only; p24 and p33 are mixed, each with four embedded verse lines. The whole-selection discovery form remains prose, with both exceptions retained at part and extent level.

formStatements.description: Discovery form for the 49-item companion remains prose. Four Middle English verse lines in p24 and four in p33 make those two items mixed-form without changing the whole-selection discovery facet.
The Mirror of Saint Edmund is prose with a four-line verse argument opening its Vernon witness stream.
Forms of Confession is prose with four embedded verse opening lines.

summaries.value: A human-readable companion selection of forty-nine primarily prose Middle English works, excerpts, translations, and witness versions historically gathered around Richard Rolle. The record keeps anonymous, disputed, source-layer, and Walter Hilton responsibilities separate and links each item through the 2003 U-M encoding and Horstmann's 1895-1896 edition to its manuscript or early-print stream.

Seven source/editorial groups contain forty-four XML DIV2 carriers but forty-nine count-once items because Narrationes Legendariae, the two Abbey works, the 1508 Remedy volume, and Profits of Tribulation each contain multiple intellectual works. Six Masters and Twelve Profits remain distinct.
